channel.title: homo-permanens on Jean-Christophe Ploquin & Arthur Gyl: two faces of an artist
channel.description: Recent content in homo-permanens on Jean-Christophe Ploquin & Arthur Gyl: two faces of an artist

item.title: 🔎 Quick Update: A Look at Three Projects
item.description: Hydrophonia – Remix 2025 The 9-minute stereo version of Hydrophonia – Remix 2025 is now available on YouTube. At the same time, the piece has been submitted to PAYSAGES | COMPOSÉS 2025, the call for experimental sound works organised by APNÉES. Jury results are expected at the end of August. Volume 4 of From Death to Life Writing continues: the manuscript has just passed the 7,000-word mark. The title is still to be decided, but the overall structure is locked in:

item.title: What if... immortality came with a price? - Volume 4 Teaser: The Rise of Homo Permanens
item.description: What if… immortality came with a price? Volume 4 Teaser: The Rise of Homo Permanens Previous volumes recap Volume 1: An ordinary man crosses the ultimate frontier and embarks on an initiatory journey that takes him to the edge of his identity and the galaxy. Questions of passage, memory, and becoming take center stage. Volume 2: Several generations later, two singular beings face the mysteries of a new environment where exploration of the world, life, and origins takes an unexpected turn.

item.title: Breaking Language Barriers: How My French SF Trilogy Found Its English Voice
item.description: Breaking Language Barriers: How My French SF Trilogy Found Its English Voice As an acousmatic music composer, I’ve always been fascinated by transformation—how sound can evolve, morph, and transcend its original form. This same fascination with metamorphosis led me to explore a profound question in science fiction: what happens when humanity itself undergoes transformation? Today, I’m thrilled to announce that my French science fiction trilogy ‘De la Mort à la Vie’ is now available worldwide in English as ‘From Death to Life.
